The move modernizes FX trading and strengthens Seoul's financial competitiveness.
Long-overdue reform positions Korea alongside global financial hubs.
editorial.stance: Editorial supports the reform as necessary modernization to enhance Korea's global financial standing.

editorials.title: Canada's submarine choice: alliances over merit?
editorials.body: Canada's decision to award its next-generation submarine program to Germany's Thyssenkrupp Marine Systems (TKMS) is more than the outcome of a comm...
editorials.pullQuote: Canada's decision to award its next-generation submarine program is more than a commercial outcome.
summary: Canada selected Germany's Thyssenkrupp for submarines, prioritizing alliance ties over competitive merit.
The decision raises questions about whether geopolitical considerations outweighed technical evaluation.
Critics argue the choice reflects diplomatic strategy rather than optimal defense procurement.
editorials.stance: Editorial questions whether Canada prioritized alliance relationships over objective technical evaluation in submarine procurement.
editorials.titleTr: 캐나다의 잠수함 선택, 동맹이 성능을 이겼나?
summaryTr: 캐나다가 경쟁 실적보다 동맹 관계를 우선시하며 독일 티센크루프의 잠수함을 선택했다.
이 결정은 지정학적 고려가 기술 평가를 앞돌았는지에 대한 의문을 제기한다.
비평가들은 이 선택이 최적의 국방 조달보다는 외교 전략을 반영한다고 주장한다.
editorials.bodyTr:
I appreciate your request, but I notice the text you've provided appears to be incomplete — it cuts off mid-sentence with "is more than the outcome of a comm..."

Could you please provide the complete editorial text? Once you share the full content, I'll be happy to translate it into natural, journalistic Korean while preserving the tone, meaning, and paragraph structure.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: The editorial presents a genuine tension between competing legitimate concerns (alliance relationships vs. merit-based procurement) without advocating clearly for either position, treating both as valid considerations in defense policy.

editorial.titleTr: Opinion: Who Gets to Decide Whether Speech Is Defamatory and Intentional?
summaryTr: The Democratic Party's amendment to the Information and Communications Network Act went into effect on the 7th.
The government and ruling party call it a law to eliminate false information, but the opposition worries it infringes on freedom of expression.
The amendment's biggest problem is the legal ambiguity in determining defamatory intent and willfulness.
editorial.bodyTr: Who gets to decide whether something is meant to disparage someone and whether it was intentional? The Democratic Party's amended Information and Communications Network Act, which was rushed through, went into effect on the 7th. The government and ruling party call it a law to eliminate false and manipulated information, but the opposition party and civic groups are raising concerns about infringement on freedom of expression. The biggest problem with the amendment is legal ambiguity...

summaryTr: Police investigation team leader faces arrest warrant for allegedly concealing evidence from murder suspect's vehicle

The police special investigation team has requested an arrest warrant for Park, a senior superintendent and head of the investigation team at the Gwangju Gwangsan Police Station, who investigated the murder case of a high school girl in Gwangju. He is suspected of concealing cable ties found inside murderer Jang Yun-gi's vehicle that were used to bind the kidnapped high school girl...

editorials.body: 사과하는 배재고 학생들에 어깨 펴라 다독인 광주일고 교장 6일 오후 광주일고 체육관. 배재고 야구부 학생들은 입장하며 시선을 들지 못했다. 광주일고 학생들 앞에 마주 앉았을 때도 잔뜩 주눅이 들어 있었다. 고개를 푹 숙이고 두 손을 모아 무릎 위에 올려놓은 학생

editorials.titleTr: School Principal Tells Apologetic Baegjae High Students to "Hold Your Head High
summaryTr: Baseball team students from Baejae High School visited Gwangju Il High School to offer an official apology.
The principal of Gwangju Il High School encouraged the intimidated students and consoled them.
A reconciliation meeting was arranged to resolve the conflict between the two schools.
editorials.bodyTr:
Gwangju Ilgo Principal Encourages Baseball Players from Baejae High to Hold Their Heads High as They Apologize

Tuesday afternoon at Gwangju Ilgo gymnasium. The baseball team members from Baejae High could not lift their eyes as they entered. Even when they sat facing the Gwangju Ilgo students, they appeared thoroughly intimidated. The students kept their heads bowed low, their clasped hands resting on their laps.

leanScore.label: Center
leanScore.rationale: 학생들의 사과와 화해를 격려하며 양측 갈등 해결을 중립적으로 다루되, 특정 집단을 비판하거나 정책 주장이 없어 명확한 좌우 지향성이 드러나지 않음.

editorial.title: [사설] 캐나다 잠수함 수주 실패, 안보협력 없는 K방산의 한계
editorial.body: 입력 지난 6월 프랑스 G7 정상회의에 참석한 이재명 대통령이 마크 카니 캐나다 총리를 만나 악수하고 있다. 캐나다는 지난 6일 차세대잠수함 도입사업의 우선협상 대상자로 한국이 아닌 같은 나토 회원국인 독일의 티센크루프마린시스템스(TKMS)를 최종 선정했다. [청와대사진기자단] 그제(6일) 최대 60조원 규모의 캐나다 차세대잠수함 도입 사업에서 한화오션이 독일 티센크루프마린시스템스(TKMS)에 고배를 마셨다. 정부와 기업이 총력전에 나섰지만 결국 캐나다는 같은 북대서양조약기구(나토) 회원국인 독일을 선택했다. 이재명 대통령은 SNS에 “오늘의 경험이 세계적인 잠수함 강국들과 당당히 경쟁한 우수한 성능과 기술력을 한층 높이는 밑거름이 될 것”이라고 밝혔다. 그러나 이번 수주 실패를 보면 잠수함 원조 국가인 독일과 대등한 경쟁을 벌였다는 사실에 만족할 수 없는 근본 원인에 주목해야 한다. 마크 카니 캐나다 총리는 독일 선택의 이유로 캐나다의 ‘전략적 안보’를 강조하면서 “나토와 완벽한 상호 운용성이 있어 합동 임무 수행이 가능하다”고 설명했다. 우크라이나 전후 세계 최대 방산시장으로 떠오른 유럽 등 나토 회원국에 가성비와 납기 준수, 산업 협력 패키지를 내세우는 기존 수출 전략이 먹히지 않는 상황이 된 것이다. 바야흐로 나토(미국과 유럽) vs 중국·러시아의 진영 대립이 본격화한 가운데, 이제 ‘무기가 우수한 나라’가 아니라 ‘무기도 우수하지만 우리 편인 나라’의 방산이 선택되는 시대가 열리고 있다. 이런 상황에서 나토에 실존 위협인 러시아를 의식해 우크라이나에 비살상무기와 인도적 지원만 하는 한국, 또한 러시아가 북한과의 군사협력을 강화하고 대북 제재를 공공연하게 반대하는데도 대응 조치를 취하지 않는 한국의 방산을 과연 나토가 어떤 시각으로 바라볼 것인지를 정부는 자문해야 한다. 나토가 대중 견제를 위해 협력 강화에 나선 인도·태평양 4개국(IP4) 가운데 호주와 뉴질랜드가 한국이 아닌 일본에 군함 생산을 맡기거나 맡기려는 것도 이런 흐름과 무관치 않다. 특히 물밑에서 진행해야 할 특사 외교를 공개리에 진행하고 구체적인 ‘선물 목록’까지 공개해 기존 K방산 최대 수입국인 동유럽 국가의 불만을 산 것도 향후 수주의 걸림돌로 작용할 수 있다. 이 대통령은 마침 나토 정상회의 참석차 터키를 방문 중이다. 나토 정상들과의 교류를 통해 최근 수주 정체 조짐을 보이는 K방산 피크 아웃의 원인을 파악해 K방산 2.0 시대의 수출 전략을 치열하게 고민하는 기회로 삼기를 바란다.
editorial.pullQuote: 나토와 완벽한 상호 운용성이 있어 합동 임무 수행이 가능하다
summary: 캐나다 60조원 잠수함 사업에서 한화오션이 독일에 패배했다.
나토는 성능보다 '우리 편'인 국가의 무기를 선택하는 추세다.
K방산은 안보협력 부재로 글로벌 시장에서 한계를 드러냈다.
editorial.stance: 한국은 무기 성능만으로는 부족하며, 진영 내 신뢰와 안보협력을 강화해야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: Editorial: Canada's Submarine Deal Collapse Exposes Limits of K-Defense Without Security Cooperation
summaryTr: Hanwha Ocean lost to Germany in Canada's 60 trillion won submarine project.
NATO tends to choose weapons from countries that are 'on our side' over performance.
South Korea's defense industry has revealed limitations in the global market due to lack of security cooperation.
editorial.bodyTr:
Hanwha Ocean lost out to Germany's ThyssenKrupp Marine Systems (TKMS) on the 6th in Canada's next-generation submarine acquisition project, valued at up to 60 trillion won. Despite an all-out effort by the government and the company, Canada ultimately chose Germany, a fellow NATO member state. President Lee Jae-myung stated on social media that "today's experience will serve as a foundation for elevating our excellent performance and technological capabilities as we compete confidently with world-class submarine powers." However, examining this procurement failure reveals a fundamental issue that cannot be overlooked: we cannot be satisfied with merely competing on equal footing with Germany, the originator of submarine technology.

Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney emphasized Canada's "strategic security" in explaining the choice of Germany, stating that "there is perfect interoperability with NATO, enabling joint mission execution." In the European defense market, which has emerged as the world's largest following the Ukraine conflict, the existing export strategy of promoting cost-effectiveness, delivery compliance, and industrial cooperation packages no longer works with NATO member states. As the confrontation between NATO (the United States and Europe) versus China and Russia intensifies, a new era is dawning where defense systems are selected not from "countries with superior weapons," but from "countries that are not only superior in weapons but are also on our side."

The government must ask itself: how will NATO view South Korean defense exports given our stance of providing only non-lethal weapons and humanitarian aid to Ukraine out of consideration for Russia, which poses a direct threat to NATO, while taking no countermeasures even as Russia strengthens military cooperation with North Korea and openly opposes sanctions against it? That Australia and New Zealand among NATO's Indo-Pacific Four (IP4) countries, established to strengthen cooperation against China, are delegating or seeking to delegate warship production to Japan rather than South Korea is not unrelated to this trend.

Particularly problematic is conducting what should be confidential special envoy diplomacy openly and publicly revealing a specific "gift list," which has already generated discontent among Eastern European countries—South Korea's largest existing defense export customers. This could become an obstacle to future procurement deals. President Lee is currently visiting Turkey to attend a NATO summit. Through dialogue with NATO leaders, this should be an opportunity to identify the causes behind the recent signs of stagnation in Korean defense exports and to carefully deliberate export strategies for the Korean defense industry 2.0 era.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: 한미동맹·나토 강화 및 러시아·북한에 대한 강경 입장을 옹호하며, 한국의 대러 약한 대응과 대북 제재 미온적 태도를 비판하는 안보-우선 프레이밍

editorial.title: Night watch
editorial.body: 24-hour FX trading should reinforce market trust without exposing the won to volatility Keeping the lights on longer does not make a market safer. It simply gives investors more hours to express confidence — or doubt. South Korea’s decision to introduce weekday 24-hour foreign exchange trading, effective from Monday, is therefore both a milestone in financial modernization and a reminder that institutional reform cannot substitute for economic strength. Opening the market around the clock is necessary. However, assuming it will stabilize the won is a costly illusion. The reform addresses a long-standing weakness in Korea's foreign exchange system. For years, the domestic market closed while global investors continued trading the won through offshore non-deliverable forward markets in London, New York and Singapore. By the time Seoul reopened, overseas price movements had already dictated the day's starting point, leaving domestic authorities to react rather than respond. According to the Bank for International Settlements, roughly 80 percent of won-forward trading last year took place through offshore NDF markets, compared with a global average of just 21 percent. Few major economies have tolerated such an imbalance for so long. Continuous trading should help correct that distortion. Overnight developments will increasingly be absorbed into the domestic market instead of arriving wholesale at the opening bell. Foreign investors will gain easier access to the won, while exporters and importers will be able to manage currency risk whenever necessary. This will help move Korea's financial infrastructure closer to international standards. The changes also support Korea's ambition to attract global capital and satisfy the remaining conditions for inclusion in the MSCI Developed Markets Index. The timing, however, is far from comfortable. The reform arrives while the won remains under exceptional pressure. The won-dollar exchange rate has hovered in the mid-1,500s for more than a month, its highest sustained level since the aftermath of the Asian financial crisis. Foreign equity outflows, expanding overseas investment and expectations of higher US interest rates continue to favor the dollar. Longer trading hours do nothing by themselves to reverse those forces. Indeed, the greatest vulnerability may emerge when the market is at its quietest. During late-night hours, trading volume will inevitably thin out. Under those conditions, relatively modest transactions or unexpected headlines could produce disproportionate price swings simply because liquidity is scarce. Large corporations can monitor currency movements around the clock. Many small and medium-sized enterprises do not. A market designed to improve efficiency should not leave smaller participants bearing greater risk simply because they lack the resources to respond overnight. That makes vigilant supervision indispensable, but surveillance alone cannot provide lasting protection. Authorities should maintain real-time monitoring capable of identifying abnormal price movements and speculative activity before instability spreads. At the same time, Korea should beef up its broader financial defenses. Stronger reserves and more durable international liquidity arrangements, including currency swap agreements where possible, would reinforce confidence during market stress. Ultimately, currencies are valued less for the hours during which they are traded than for the economies behind them. Investors hold a currency because they trust a country’s institutions, policy direction and long-term growth prospects. Extending trading hours modernizes the mechanics of the market, but it does not alter those fundamentals. Sustainable exchange-rate stability will depend on policies that strengthen productivity, encourage investment, improve labor market flexibility and deepen confidence in Korea's economic future. Keeping dealing rooms open through the night is an important step toward a more integrated financial market. But markets, like investors, never reward lost sleep for its own sake. They reward confidence. Whether this reform delivers stability or volatility will depend less on the clock than on whether the economy gives investors reason to believe after dark. summary: South Korea launches 24-hour weekday FX trading to reduce reliance on offshore won markets and modernize its financial infrastructure.
Extended hours alone won't stabilize the won amid dollar pressure; thin liquidity during off-peak times risks disproportionate price swings.
Real economic strength—not trading hours—determines currency stability; authorities must strengthen reserves and boost productivity for lasting confidence.
editorial.stance: Welcome necessary market modernization, but recognize that institutional reforms cannot substitute for underlying economic fundamentals and must be paired with vigilant oversight.
summaryTr: 한국이 역외 원화 시장 의존도를 낮추고 금융 인프라를 현대화하기 위해 평일 24시간 외환 거래를 시작했다.
거래 시간 연장만으로는 달러 압박 속에서 원화를 안정화하기 어렵고, 비거래 시간대의 얇은 유동성은 불균형한 가격 변동을 초래할 위험이 있다.
실제 경제 강도야말로 통화 안정성을 결정하는 요소이며, 당국은 외환보유액을 강화하고 생산성을 높여 지속적인 신뢰를 구축해야 한다.
editorial.bodyTr:
24시간 외환거래가 시장 신뢰를 강화하되 원화 변동성은 차단해야

시장을 더 오래 열어놓는다고 해서 시장이 더 안전해지는 것은 아니다. 단지 투자자들이 신뢰나 의심을 드러낼 수 있는 시간만 늘어날 뿐이다. 월요일부터 시행되는 한국의 평일 24시간 외환거래 도입은 따라서 금융 현대화의 이정표인 동시에 제도 개혁이 경제적 강건함을 대체할 수 없다는 점을 상기시킨다. 시장을 24시간 운영하는 것은 필요하다. 하지만 이것이 원화를 안정시킬 것이라고 가정하는 것은 비용이 큰 착각이다.

이번 개혁은 한국 외환시장의 오랫동안 지적되어 온 약점을 해결한다. 수년간 국내시장이 문을 닫아 있는 동안 국제 투자자들은 런던, 뉴욕, 싱가포르의 오프쇼어 선도환(NDF) 시장을 통해 원화 거래를 계속해왔다. 서울이 시장을 다시 열 때쯤이면 해외 가격 변동이 이미 그날의 시작점을 결정하고 있어 국내 당국은 대응이 아닌 반응만 할 수 있었다. 국제결제은행에 따르면 지난해 원화 선도환 거래의 약 80%가 오프쇼어 NDF 시장에서 이루어졌으며 이는 글로벌 평균 21%와 비교된다. 이러한 불균형을 이렇게 오랫동안 용인해온 주요 경제국은 거의 없다. 연속 거래는 이러한 왜곡을 바로잡는 데 도움이 될 것이다. 야간 변동이 개장 때 한꺼번에 몰려오지 않고 국내시장에서 점진적으로 흡수될 것이다. 해외 투자자들은 원화에 더 쉽게 접근할 수 있게 되고 수출입 기업들은 필요할 때마다 환율 위험을 관리할 수 있다. 이는 한국의 금융 인프라를 국제 수준에 한층 가깝게 만드는 데 도움이 될 것이다. 이번 변화는 또한 글로벌 자본 유입을 확대하고 MSCI 선진국지수 편입의 남은 조건을 충족하려는 한국의 야심을 뒷받침한다.

그러나 현재의 시점은 결코 좋지 않다. 이번 개혁은 원화가 사상 유례없는 압박을 받고 있는 시기에 추진된다. 원달러 환율이 1,500원대 중반에서 1개월 이상 머물고 있으며 이는 아시아 금융위기 직후 이후 가장 높은 수준이다. 해외 주식 자금 유출, 해외 투자 확대, 미국 금리 인상 예상이 계속해서 달러를 선호하고 있다. 거래 시간 연장 자체로는 이러한 흐름을 역전시킬 수 없다.

가장 큰 취약점은 시장이 가장 조용할 때 나타날 수 있다. 야간 시간대에는 거래량이 필연적으로 줄어든다. 이러한 조건에서는 상대적으로 작은 규모의 거래나 예상치 못한 뉴스도 유동성 부족으로 인해 과도한 가격 변동을 초래할 수 있다. 대형 기업들은 24시간 환율 변동을 모니터링할 수 있다. 많은 중소기업들은 그렇지 못하다. 효율성을 높이기 위해 설계된 시장이 더 작은 규모의 기업 참가자들에게 야간 대응 자원 부족으로 인해 더 큰 위험을 안겨서는 안 된다. 이는 철저한 감시가 필수적임을 의미하지만 감시 활동만으로는 지속적인 보호를 제공할 수 없다.

당국은 비정상적 가격 변동과 투기 활동을 불안정성이 확산되기 전에 파악할 수 있는 실시간 모니터링을 유지해야 한다. 동시에 한국은 더 광범위한 금융 방어 태세를 강화해야 한다. 더 강력한 외환보유액과 가능한 한 통화스왑협정을 포함한 더 지속 가능한 국제 유동성 체계는 시장 스트레스 상황에서 신뢰를 강화할 것이다.

궁극적으로 통화는 거래되는 시간대보다는 그 뒤에 있는 경제를 위해 가치를 평가받는다. 투자자들이 통화를 보유하는 것은 한 국가의 제도, 정책 방향, 장기 성장 전망을 신뢰하기 때문이다. 거래 시간을 연장하는 것은 시장의 메커니즘을 현대화하지만 이러한 기본 요소는 바꾸지 않는다. 지속 가능한 환율 안정은 생산성을 강화하고 투자를 장려하며 노동시장 유연성을 개선하고 한국의 경제 미래에 대한 신뢰를 심화하는 정책에 달려 있다. 밤새 딜링룸을 열어놓는 것은 더욱 통합된 금융시장을 향한 중요한 발걸음이다. 하지만 시장은 투자자와 마찬가지로 자고 지낸 시간에 대해 보상해주지 않는다. 신뢰에 대해 보상해줄 뿐이다. 이번 개혁이 안정성을 가져올지 변동성을 가져올지는 시계 바늘보다는 경제가 어둠이 내린 후에도 투자자들에게 믿음의 이유를 제공하는지에 달려 있다.

editorials.title: Night watch
editorials.body: 24-hour FX trading should reinforce market trust without exposing the won to volatility Keeping the lights on longer does not make a market safer. It simply gives investors more hours to express confidence — or doubt. South Korea’s decision to introduce weekday 24-hour foreign exchange trading, effective from Monday, is therefore both a milestone in financial modernization and a reminder that institutional reform cannot substitute for economic strength. Opening the market around the clock is necessary. However, assuming it will stabilize the won is a costly illusion. The reform addresses a long-standing weakness in Korea's foreign exchange system. For years, the domestic market closed while global investors continued trading the won through offshore non-deliverable forward markets in London, New York and Singapore. By the time Seoul reopened, overseas price movements had already dictated the day's starting point, leaving domestic authorities to react rather than respond. According to the Bank for International Settlements, roughly 80 percent of won-forward trading last year took place through offshore NDF markets, compared with a global average of just 21 percent. Few major economies have tolerated such an imbalance for so long. Continuous trading should help correct that distortion. Overnight developments will increasingly be absorbed into the domestic market instead of arriving wholesale at the opening bell. Foreign investors will gain easier access to the won, while exporters and importers will be able to manage currency risk whenever necessary. This will help move Korea's financial infrastructure closer to international standards. The changes also support Korea's ambition to attract global capital and satisfy the remaining conditions for inclusion in the MSCI Developed Markets Index. The timing, however, is far from comfortable. The reform arrives while the won remains under exceptional pressure. The won-dollar exchange rate has hovered in the mid-1,500s for more than a month, its highest sustained level since the aftermath of the Asian financial crisis. Foreign equity outflows, expanding overseas investment and expectations of higher US interest rates continue to favor the dollar. Longer trading hours do nothing by themselves to reverse those forces. Indeed, the greatest vulnerability may emerge when the market is at its quietest. During late-night hours, trading volume will inevitably thin out. Under those conditions, relatively modest transactions or unexpected headlines could produce disproportionate price swings simply because liquidity is scarce. Large corporations can monitor currency movements around the clock. Many small and medium-sized enterprises do not. A market designed to improve efficiency should not leave smaller participants bearing greater risk simply because they lack the resources to respond overnight. That makes vigilant supervision indispensable, but surveillance alone cannot provide lasting protection. Authorities should maintain real-time monitoring capable of identifying abnormal price movements and speculative activity before instability spreads. At the same time, Korea should beef up its broader financial defenses. Stronger reserves and more durable international liquidity arrangements, including currency swap agreements where possible, would reinforce confidence during market stress. Ultimately, currencies are valued less for the hours during which they are traded than for the economies behind them. Investors hold a currency because they trust a country’s institutions, policy direction and long-term growth prospects. Extending trading hours modernizes the mechanics of the market, but it does not alter those fundamentals. Sustainable exchange-rate stability will depend on policies that strengthen productivity, encourage investment, improve labor market flexibility and deepen confidence in Korea's economic future. Keeping dealing rooms open through the night is an important step toward a more integrated financial market. But markets, like investors, never reward lost sleep for its own sake. They reward confidence. Whether this reform delivers stability or volatility will depend less on the clock than on whether the economy gives investors reason to believe after dark. summary: South Korea launches 24-hour weekday FX trading to reduce reliance on offshore won markets and modernize its financial infrastructure.
Extended hours alone won't stabilize the won amid dollar pressure; thin liquidity during off-peak times risks disproportionate price swings.
Real economic strength—not trading hours—determines currency stability; authorities must strengthen reserves and boost productivity for lasting confidence.
editorials.stance: Welcome necessary market modernization, but recognize that institutional reforms cannot substitute for underlying economic fundamentals and must be paired with vigilant oversight.
summaryTr: 한국이 역외 원화 시장 의존도를 낮추고 금융 인프라를 현대화하기 위해 평일 24시간 외환 거래를 시작했다.
거래 시간 연장만으로는 달러 압박 속에서 원화를 안정화하기 어렵고, 비거래 시간대의 얇은 유동성은 불균형한 가격 변동을 초래할 위험이 있다.
실제 경제 강도야말로 통화 안정성을 결정하는 요소이며, 당국은 외환보유액을 강화하고 생산성을 높여 지속적인 신뢰를 구축해야 한다.

editorials.titleTr: Police Destroyed Murder Evidence in Supplementary Investigation, Says Prosecution ... Did Ruling Party See It?
summaryTr: Supplementary investigation by prosecutors in the Gwangju schoolgirl murder case revealed evidence destruction by police.
Evidence destruction by the suspect's father, an active-duty police officer, and police negligence and cover-up were uncovered.
Prosecutors uncovered sexual assault charges and sought life imprisonment or capital punishment.
editorials.bodyTr: An incident in Gwangju demonstrates why prosecutors' supplementary investigative authority is a crucial safeguard for criminal justice. The prosecution's supplementary investigation into the Jang Yun-gi case, in which police had referred a high school girl's murder suspect as a simple homicide, revealed shocking details. Evidence destruction by the suspect's father, a sitting police officer, as well as negligent police investigation and cover-up, all came to light one after another. The prosecution uncovered circumstances indicating the crime was committed for purposes of sexual assault, leading to severe punishment of life imprisonment or capital punishment.

editorials.title: [사설] 삼성전자 2분기 영업이익 89조…안주 말고 초격차 투자 나서야
editorials.body: [사설] 삼성전자 2분기 영업이익 89조…안주 말고 초격차 투자 나서야, 오피니언
summary: 삼성전자 2분기 영업이익 89조원으로 호실적을 기록했다.
현실에 안주하지 말고 초격차 기술 투자에 나서야 한다.
미래 경쟁력 확보를 위한 과감한 전략 전환이 필요하다.
editorials.stance: 현재의 실적에만 머물지 말고 미래를 대비한 선제적 투자 확대를 촉구
editorials.titleTr: Samsung Electronics' Q2 Operating Profit at 8.9 Trillion Won…Time to Invest in Game-Changing Innovation, Not Complacency
summaryTr: Samsung Electronics reported strong earnings with operating profit of 8.9 trillion won in the second quarter.
The company must not settle for the status quo and should pursue investments in cutting-edge technology.
Bold strategic shifts are necessary to secure competitive advantages for the future.
